LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

Defendants Rasier, Llc; Rasier-Ca, Llc; Uber Technologies, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Carter Zinn (415-292-4100) of The Zinn Law Firm for Plaintiff, Vikram Kashyap.
Sabina Sadykhova (sabina.sadykhova@wilsonelser.com) of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP for Defendants Rasier, LLC, Rasier-CA, LLC and Uber Technologies, Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS RASIER LLC, RASIER-CA LLC, AND UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From First Amended Complaint.

Defendants Rasier, LLC, Rasier-CA, LLC, and Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion for an order to strike punitive damages from the First Amended Complaint as to these Defendants is granted without leave to amend.

[see word minute for details]

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Viktar Sviatlichny?S Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. JEREMY I. POLLACK, Esq. and T. ANDREW DAVIES, Esq. of The Zinn Law Firm
(Tel: 415-292-4100) for Plaintiff Vikram Kashyap;
Atty. JULIE L. BATZ, Esq. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP
(Tel: 510-540-4881) for Defendant Viktar Sviatlichny.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT VIKTAR SVIATLICHNY's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Viktar Sviatlichny's "motion to strike punitive damages from the first amended complaint" (FAC) is denied. The FAC pleads that plaintiff Vikram Kashyap was riding a bicycle on Bush Street when a passenger exiting Sviatlichny's Prius suddenly opened a door into Kashyap's path, causing a collision. (FAC 3:17-27; 19:10-25.) Sviatlichny then allegedly fled the scene, failing to provide aid and/or information to Kashyap. (Id.) This suffices to plead a basis for punitive damages.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
